tool_results.output:
1	# Agentic Primitives
2	
3	> Atomic building blocks for AI agent systems
4	
5	[![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-3.1.2-purple.svg)](VERSION)
6	[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](LICENSE)
7	[![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.11+-blue.svg)](https://www.python.org/)
8	
9	---
10	
11	## What Are Agentic Primitives?
12	
13	Atomic building blocks for AI agent systems — packaged as Claude Code plugins and Python libraries.
14	
15	Designed to work in two contexts:
16	- **Human-in-the-loop** — a developer using Claude Code in the terminal, invoking commands explicitly, staying in control
17	- **Headless workspaces** — fully automated agents running in isolation with no human present, where tool scoping and policy hooks are the safety layer
18	
19	The same primitives serve both. The difference is configuration: which tools are allowed, which hooks fire, which agents are trusted.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## The Primitives
24	
25	### Commands
26	Explicit user-invocable slash commands (`/sdlc:git_push`). Granular, predictable, human-triggered. The developer stays in the loop.
27	
28	→ Lives in: `plugins/<plugin>/commands/<name>.md`
29	
30	### Skills
31	Reusable workflows Claude invokes automatically when a task matches the description — or that you invoke as `/sdlc:git`. Consolidated, intent-driven, works in both human and headless contexts.
32	
33	→ Lives in: `plugins/<plugin>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`
34	
35	### Agents
36	Named specialist subagents with a scoped system prompt, explicit allowed/disallowed tools, and optional persistent memory. The tool scope is the key primitive — it determines what an agent *can* do, not just what it *should* do. Agents can preload skills and delegate to other agents via the `Task` tool.
37	
38	→ Lives in: `plugins/<plugin>/agents/<name>/agent.md`
39	
40	### Hooks
41	Event-driven automation that fires on Claude Code lifecycle events (`PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `SubagentStop`, `SessionStart`, etc.). Observe, modify, or block — enforcing policies and emitting telemetry without touching workflow code.
42	
43	→ Lives in: `plugins/<plugin>/hooks/hooks.json` + handlers
44	
45	### Lib
46	Python packages that power agent runtimes — isolation, events, logging, security. Used by the [Agentic Engineering Framework (AEF)](https://github.com/AgentParadise/agentic-engineering-framework) as its foundation.
47	
48	→ Lives in: `lib/python/`
49	
50	---
51	
52	### How They Compose
53	
54	```
55	User: /sdlc:git_push          ← Command (explicit, human-in-loop)
56	  or
57	Claude detects push needed    ← Skill (auto-invoked, headless-friendly)
58	       │
59	       ├─► PreToolUse Hook validates git commands before execution
60	       │
61	       ├─► Skill delegates review to env-reviewer Agent (Task tool)
62	       │         ├─ tools: Read, Grep, Glob only (cannot modify anything)
63	       │         ├─ disallowedTools: Write, Edit (enforced, not just instructed)
64	       │         └─ SubagentStop Hook records telemetry
65	       │
66	       └─► PostToolUse Hook emits structured JSONL event (Lib: agentic-events)
67	```
68	
69	**The pattern:**
70	- **Commands** give humans direct control at the right granularity
71	- **Skills** orchestrate work for agents — consolidated, intent-driven
72	- **Agents** specialize with enforced tool scopes — least privilege by design
73	- **Hooks** enforce policies and observability without touching workflow code
74	- **Lib** provides the runtime substrate — isolation, events, structured logging
75	
76	---
77	
78	## Quick Start
79	
80	### Prerequisites
81	
82	- [Python 3.11+](https://www.python.org/)
83	- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) — fast Python package manager
84	- [just](https://github.com/casey/just) — command runner (optional, recommended)
85	
86	### Install Plugins
87	
88	Plugins are installed via Claude Code's built-in plugin system. Requires Claude Code v1.0.33+.
89	
90	You can also do all of this interactively by typing `/plugin` inside Claude Code.
91	
92	**1. Add the marketplace (one-time setup):**
93	
94	```bash
95	claude plugin marketplace add AgentParadise/agentic-primitives
96	```
97	
98	**2. Install the plugins you need:**
99	
100	```bash
101	# Install globally (available in all projects)
102	claude plugin install sdlc@agentic-primitives --scope user
103	
104	# Or install to current project only
105	claude plugin install sdlc@agentic-primitives --scope project
106	```
107	
108	**3. Update to the latest version:**
109	
110	```bash
111	# Refresh the marketplace catalog first
112	claude plugin marketplace update agentic-primitives
113	
114	# Then update the plugin
115	claude plugin update sdlc@agentic-primitives
116	```
117	
118	Plugins are pinned to a version and never auto-update. Updates require both steps above.
119	
120	**4. Disable / enable without uninstalling:**
121	
122	```bash
123	claude plugin disable sdlc@agentic-primitives
124	claude plugin enable sdlc@agentic-primitives
125	```
126	
127	**5. Uninstall:**
128	
129	```bash
130	claude plugin uninstall sdlc@agentic-primitives
131	```
132	
133	**6. Verify security hooks are active:**
134	
135	```bash
136	# Inside a Claude Code session, run:
137	/sdlc:validate_security-hooks
138	```
139	
140	Replace `sdlc` with any plugin name from the [Available Plugins](#available-plugins) table in the commands above.
141	
142	---
143	
144	## Available Plugins
145	
146	| Plugin | Install | Description |
147	|--------|---------|-------------|
148	| **sdlc** | `claude plugin install sdlc@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Software Development Lifecycle |
149	| **workspace** | `claude plugin install workspace@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Observable isolated workspaces |
150	| **research** | `claude plugin install research@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Information gathering |
151	| **meta** | `claude plugin install meta@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Primitive generators |
152	| **docs** | `claude plugin install docs@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Documentation tools |
153	| **notifications** | `claude plugin install notifications@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Push notifications (ntfy, macOS, Pushover) |
154	| **observability** | `claude plugin install observability@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Full-spectrum JSONL event observability |
155	| **delegation** | `claude plugin install delegation@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Delegating work — `claude -p`, Codex, and session handoffs |
156	| **experiments** | `claude plugin install experiments@agentic-primitives --scope user` | Hypothesis-first experiment workflow |
157	
158	### What's in each plugin
159	
160	| Plugin | Commands | Skills | Agents | Hooks |
161	|--------|----------|--------|--------|-------|
162	| **sdlc** | `git_push`, `git_merge`, `git_merge-cycle`, `git_fetch`, `git_worktree`, `git_set-attributions`, `review`, `validate_security-hooks`, `browser`, `browser_ui-review` | `git`, `git-worktree`, `commit`, `pre-commit-qa`, `qa-setup`, `testing-expert`, `review`, `prioritize`, `env-management`, `centralized-configuration`, `macos-keychain-secrets`, `browser` | `env-reviewer`, `browser-qa-agent` | PreToolUse security validators, UserPromptSubmit PII detection, git hooks |
163	| **workspace** | -- | -- | -- | Session lifecycle, tool observability, structured JSONL event emission |
164	| **research** | `scrape_docs` | -- | -- | -- |
165	| **meta** | `create-command`, `create-prime`, `create-doc-sync` | `prompt-generator` | -- | -- |
166	| **docs** | -- | `fuma` (Fumadocs integration), `system-infographic` | -- | -- |
167	| **notifications** | -- | -- | -- | Notification, Stop, TaskCompleted → ntfy/macOS/Pushover with sound themes |
168	| **observability** | -- | -- | -- | All 14 lifecycle events → structured JSONL via agentic_events |
169	| **delegation** | -- | `delegating-to-claude-p`, `delegating-to-codex`, `writing-handoffs` | -- | -- |
170	| **experiments** | -- | `running-experiments` | -- | -- |
171	
172	---
173	
174	## Python Packages
175	
176	Infrastructure primitives in `lib/python/`, installable via `pip` or `uv`:
177	
178	| Package | Version | Description |
179	|---------|---------|-------------|
180	| [`agentic-isolation`](lib/python/agentic_isolation/) | 0.3.0 | Docker workspace sandboxing for agent execution |
181	| [`agentic-events`](lib/python/agentic_events/) | 0.1.0 | Zero-dependency JSONL event emission |
182	| [`agentic-logging`](lib/python/agentic_logging/) | 0.1.0 | Structured logging for agents and humans |
183	
184	```bash
185	# Install a package for development
186	cd lib/python/agentic_isolation
187	uv sync --all-extras
188	
189	# Run tests
190	uv run pytest -x -q
191	```
192	
193	---
194	
195	## Repository Structure
196	
197	```
198	agentic-primitives/
199	├── plugins/                    # Prompt Primitives
200	│   ├── sdlc/                   #   SDLC plugin
201	│   │   ├── commands/           #     Explicit user-invocable slash commands
202	│   │   ├── skills/             #     Agent-invocable workflows
203	│   │   ├── agents/             #     Named subagents with scoped tools
204	│   │   └── hooks/              #     Lifecycle event handlers
205	│   ├── workspace/              #   Workspace observability hooks
206	│   ├── research/               #   Research tools (firecrawl, doc-scraper)
207	│   ├── meta/                   #   Primitive generators
208	│   └── docs/                   #   Documentation tools
209	├── lib/python/                 # Infrastructure Primitives
210	│   ├── agentic_isolation/      #   Docker workspace sandboxing
211	│   ├── agentic_events/         #   JSONL event emission
212	│   └── agentic_logging/        #   Structured logging
213	├── providers/                  # Workspace providers & model data
214	│   ├── workspaces/claude-cli/  #   Claude CLI Docker workspace
215	│   ├── models/                 #   Model cards (pricing, context windows)
216	│   └── agents/                 #   Agent configuration templates
217	├── scripts/                    # QA runner, benchmark tools
218	├── tests/                      # Integration & unit tests
219	├── docs/adrs/                  # Architecture Decision Records (32 ADRs)
220	├── VERSION                     # Repo version (3.0.1)
221	└── justfile                    # Task runner (just --list)
222	```
223	
224	---
225	
226	## Development
227	
228	```bash
229	# Initialize environment
230	just init
231	
232	# Run all tests
233	just test
234	
235	# Run QA (format check + lint + test)
236	just qa
237	
238	# Auto-fix formatting and lint issues
239	just qa-fix
240	
241	# Run full CI pipeline
242	just ci
243	```
244	
245	### Docker Workspace Images
246	
247	```bash
248	# Build Claude CLI workspace image
249	just build-workspace-claude-cli
250	
251	# List available providers
252	just list-providers
253	```
254	
255	---
256	
257	## Workspace
258	
259	`agentic-primitives` ships the workspace image — the controlled boundary every AI agent runs inside. The workspace has three responsibilities:
260	
261	1. **Inject** orchestrator-supplied context (`CLAUDE.md`, plugins, subagents) via a bind-mount at `/etc/agentic/workspace/` + three optional env vars (`AGENTIC_WORKSPACE_CONTEXT`, `AGENTIC_WORKSPACE_PLUGINS`, `AGENTIC_WORKSPACE_AGENTS`).
262	2. **Isolate** the agent's effects (tmpfs home, read-only context mount, network whitelisting, per-task volumes).
263	3. **Observe** what the agent did (git hooks → JSONL on stderr, `--output-format stream-json` on stdout, output artifacts in `/workspace/artifacts/output/`).
264	
265	See [`docs/workspace.md`](docs/workspace.md) for the canonical reference, [`docs/adrs/035-workspace-injection-contract.md`](docs/adrs/035-workspace-injection-contract.md) for the design decisions, and [`providers/workspaces/claude-cli/scripts/entrypoint.sh`](providers/workspaces/claude-cli/scripts/entrypoint.sh) for the source of truth.
266	
267	---
268	
269	## Architecture Decision Records
270	
271	This project's design decisions are documented in [13 ADRs](docs/adrs/), including:
272	
273	- [ADR-020: Agentic Prompt Taxonomy](docs/adrs/020-agentic-prompt-taxonomy.md)
274	- [ADR-025: Just Task Runner](docs/adrs/025-just-task-runner.md)
275	- [ADR-027: Provider Workspace Images](docs/adrs/027-provider-workspace-images.md)
276	- [ADR-029: Simplified Event System](docs/adrs/029-simplified-event-system.md)
277	- [ADR-033: Plugin-Native Workspace Images](docs/adrs/033-plugin-native-workspace-images.md)
278	- [ADR-035: Workspace Injection Contract](docs/adrs/035-workspace-injection-contract.md)
279	
280	---
281	
282	## License
283	
284	[MIT](LICENSE)
285

259	`agentic-primitives` ships the workspace image — the controlled boundary every AI agent runs inside. The workspace has three responsibilities:
260	
261	1. **Inject** orchestrator-supplied context (`CLAUDE.md`, plugins, subagents) via a bind-mount at `/etc/agentic/workspace/` + three optional env vars (`AGENTIC_WORKSPACE_CONTEXT`, `AGENTIC_WORKSPACE_PLUGINS`, `AGENTIC_WORKSPACE_AGENTS`).
262	2. **Isolate** the agent's effects (tmpfs home, read-only context mount, network whitelisting, per-task volumes).
263	3. **Observe** what the agent did (git hooks → JSONL on stderr, `--output-format stream-json` on stdout, output artifacts in `/workspace/artifacts/output/`).
264	
265	See [`docs/workspace.md`](docs/workspace.md) for the canonical reference, [`docs/adrs/035-workspace-injection-contract.md`](docs/adrs/035-workspace-injection-contract.md) for the design decisions, and [`providers/workspaces/claude-cli/scripts/entrypoint.sh`](providers/workspaces/claude-cli/scripts/entrypoint.sh) for the source of truth.
266
